context: Existing project, fresh root. STATE_JSON is a valid bruhs state (stack, tooling, integrations.linear). A caller from an older bruhs version (or a third-party automation) still pipes JSON to write_bruhs_config.py expecting it to write .claude/bruhs.json.
expected_behavior: Exit code matches what sync_bruhs_block.py --kind state would produce — 0 on success, 2 on validation error, 3 on filesystem error
Stderr contains a deprecation warning naming write_bruhs_config.py and pointing at sync_bruhs_block.py — visible to humans, not buried
Despite the script being named 'write_bruhs_config.py', it writes the bruhs:state block to CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md, NOT .claude/bruhs.json
After success, .claude/bruhs.json does NOT appear at the project root
Stdout matches sync_bruhs_block.py's stdout (one path per line of the written marker files) so old callers parsing stdout still get a path
--root flag still works and is forwarded to the underlying script
--dry-run still works and is forwarded — does not touch disk
Validation errors are produced by the underlying sync script (same shape, same exit codes), not silently swallowed
failure_modes: Still writes .claude/bruhs.json as a side effect — undermines the migration
Swallows the deprecation warning — old callers never learn to migrate
Crashes with ModuleNotFoundError because sync_bruhs_block.py isn't located relative to the shim
Skips validation — accepts invalid JSON that the underlying script would reject
Stdout output differs from sync_bruhs_block.py output, breaking automated callers that grep the path
--dry-run actually writes to disk because the flag isn't forwarded
